title: The effect of speech masking on the human subcortical response to continuous speech
description: This dataset comprises auditory brainstem response (ABR) recordings from 25 normal-hearing participants exposed to continuous speech under varying masking conditions. Participants listened to peaky speech from one to five simultaneous talkers at different signal-to-noise ratios (clean, 0 dB, -3 dB, -6 dB SNR), as well as click trains, while EEG was recorded. The study investigates how speech masking affects subcortical neural encoding of naturally produced speech in human listeners.
methods_description: EEG recordings were collected from 25 participants (ages 19-37 years) with normal hearing using a 48 kHz sampling rate via ER-2 insert earphones. Stimuli included randomized click trains (40 Hz average rate, 10 minutes total) and peaky speech from male narrators (30 minutes per SNR condition). Participants sat in a sound-isolating booth while stimuli were presented at 65 dB SPL (71 dB for 5 talkers). Data were recorded in Brain Products format and include stimulus files with audio and regressors for deconvolution analysis.
